character.chronicle: MINDS EYE THEATER ~ PILLARS OF SALT
character.title: Apprentice 4

Print Sheet: MINDS EYE THEATER ~ PILLARS OF SALT
Talisman: A large scrimshaw you had carved in the Dutch East Indies with native Indonesian and Islamic sigils.

rows: Your family had long been involved in the overseas trade that made the Netherlands the economic power it was, and your father was an intstrumental member of the Dutch East India Company. While your mother may have preferred you to sit safely behind a desk, you took to the seas as a boy alongside your father, learning the craft of trade and the matters of the sea. When you came of age, you began commanding your own ships sailing to what is now Indonesia, dealing with company business. While the business itself was prosaic enough, the travel certainly wasn't, and pirates and others often threatened to lay siege to your ships. You knew of many who were lost to the combination of weather and piracy, and had longed looked for ways to better ensure your trips. Strange it should happen into your lap one day, without you even suspecting it. You'd purchased the lot of old tomes more as a gift for your father, who appreciated them, but while thumbing through the pages of several of the texts, you found out they were, in fact, magic. What was more, you could do the magic in the books. It was accidental, at first, but as you continued to read, you continued to do things, and felt yourself come alive in ways you didn't know you could before. You began performing most of the basics just to keep your ship protected while at sea, discretely of course, but soon found yourself even using some of the rituals to further your own business interests. Your abilities drew interest from several sources, but the first to get to you were the Tremere, who had chantries even in Indonesia. They embraced you quickly, eager to have a natural but untrained mage in their midsts, particularly one with your sort of influence. Unfortunately, their interest in you and your book were not the only ones. No sooner were you embraced than you made the aquaintance of Fareed, a Setite who found you infinetely fascinating. She herself was a sorcereress, and promised you much knowledge, and even offered to help you understand the strange magical texts you had in hand. Unfortunately, you didn't heed your clan's warnings, and you fell under her spell, drawn into her whispers and promises, and addicted to her blood. When she gained what she sought, she left you to face the discipline of your clan and the loss of the rare texts which had made you valuable to them. Still, you used it as a lesson: Never trust a Setite sorcerer. You vowed to hunt down all those you came across and to find your lost texts. In the meantime, you've worked hard to regain your lost face. Your position in Indonesia put you in contact with both the Courts of the Ashirra and the strange Cathayans, giving you knowledge of both, something that has been invaluable these late nights. You've most recently been in Amsterdam, your native soil, working with the chantry there to share your information and insight, but the clan has transferred you to a new domain: Los Angeles. There are those who are nervous with the clan politics in the area, particularly Uleg Bey who is becoming a force within the clan at large, much to the chagrin of many in Western Europe who support Anastasz di Zagreb, the Justicar. But Bey's rise also has the approval of certain elders in Europe, ones who feel that Justicar Di Zagreb has not done enough in the face of the growing Cathayan threat nor in addressing the fall of the Hong Kong chantry and the defection of Oliver Thrace. Becuase of your location for the last century (Jakarta, Indonesia), and because of your knowledge, you are being deliberately transfered to the Los Angeles Chantry, a city where Bey seems to have put much of his influence. While you are just a neonate without much influence, you are one who has much knowledge, and perhaps can tip the tide, one way or the other, with the politics of this situation. Besides, rumor has it that the very text you've been seeking might be in the area, and may be easily able to be procurred by a Setite who someone says may be hiding in Los Angeles. Perhaps you can find a way to redeem yourself yet.
Your relationship to these people is described. This may mean they do not know you.
